info.title: EssayCoach API v2
info.description:
Modern async API for EssayCoach - AI-powered essay analysis platform.

    This API uses Django Ninja for type-safe, high-performance endpoints.

    ## Authentication
    All endpoints require authentication via:
    - **JWT Bearer Authentication**: `Authorization: Bearer <access-token>`
    - **Session Authentication**: For browser-based access

    ## Features
    - **AI Feedback**: Essay analysis and feedback generation
    - **Authentication**: User management and authentication
    - **Core**: Classes, rubrics, tasks, submissions, feedback

    ## Migration Status
    This is the new v2 API. The legacy v1 API (DRF) is still available at `/api/v1/`.

post.summary: Run the Essay Agent Dify workflow
post.description:
Triggers the Essay Agent workflow using Dify. Provide `essay_question` and
    `essay_content`; the API automatically handles rubric upload.

    Optional `language` and `response_mode` (blocking or streaming) are supported.
    Uses EssayAgentInterface for provider-agnostic architecture.

Send a message to AI and get a response about the essay.

    This endpoint uses Dify's chat-messages API to provide conversational
    feedback about essays. The context field can include essay_id, previous
    feedback, or any relevant context for the conversation.

get.description:
Get the status and results of a workflow run by its ID.

    This endpoint polls Dify to check the current status of a workflow execution.
    Returns status, outputs, error message, and timing information.
Get current user info using JWT authentication.

post.description: Login and receive JWT access + refresh tokens.

Refresh access token using refresh token.

This endpoint implements token rotation - a new refresh token is issued
each time, and the old one is blacklisted for security.

get.description:
Get current user info.
Used by frontend to check authentication status.

post.description:
Logout by blacklisting the refresh token.

This invalidates the refresh token, preventing further token refreshes.

get.description:
Get current user's preferences.

Returns user preferences with defaults for any missing keys.

put.description:
Update current user's preferences.

Only provided fields will be updated. Other preferences remain unchanged.

post.description:
Upload user avatar.

Accepts image files (PNG, JPG, JPEG). Max size: 5MB.
Avatar is stored in MEDIA_ROOT/avatars/<user_id>_<filename>

get.description:
Get list of active sessions for current user.

Returns all active Django sessions with device and IP information.

delete.description:
Revoke a specific session.

This will log out the user from that session/device.
Cannot revoke the current session.

get.description:
Get login history for current user.

Returns recent login attempts (success and failed) from Django session data.
Note: This is a basic implementation. For production, consider using
django-axes or similar for comprehensive login tracking.
Legacy role-aware dashboard endpoint used by existing tests/clients.

Get a specific user.

Permissions:
- Admin/Lecturer: Can view any user
- Student: Can only view themselves

put.description:
Update a user.

Permissions:
- Admin: Can update any user
- Lecturer: Can update any user except admins
- Student: Can only update themselves

delete.description:
Delete a user.

Permissions:
- Admin: Can delete any user (except other admins)
- Lecturer/Student: Cannot delete users

get.description:
Get user statistics including essay count, average score, and activity.

Permissions:
- Any authenticated user can view their own stats
- Lecturers/Admins can view any user's stats

Get user's earned badges.

Permissions:
- Any authenticated user can view their own badges
- Lecturers/Admins can view any user's badges
- Students can view other students' badges (for social learning)

Get user's progress over time (weekly or monthly aggregation).

Query params:
- period: 'week' or 'month' (default: 'month')

Returns time-series data with essay count and average score per period.

Permissions:
- Any authenticated user can view their own progress
- Lecturers/Admins can view any user's progress

post.description: Student joins a class using join code.

get.description: Get all students in a class.

post.description: Add a student to a class (admin/lecturer only).

delete.description: Remove a student from a class (admin/lecturer only).

post.description: Archive a class.

Batch enroll students via email. Creates 'unregistered' users if they don't exist.
Admin only.

Invite a new lecturer via email. Creates an 'unregistered' lecturer account.
Admin only.
Publish a task (lecturer/admin only).
Unpublish a task (lecturer/admin only).

get.description: Get all submissions for a task.

post.description: Duplicate an existing task. Admins and lecturers only.
Extend the deadline for a specific task. Admins and lecturers only.

get.description:
List rubrics with visibility-based filtering.

- Public rubrics: Visible to all authenticated users
- Private rubrics: Only visible to creator or admin
- Students: Only see public rubrics
- Admins: See all rubrics (public and private)

Query params:
- visibility=public|private: Filter by visibility (overrides default role-based filtering)

post.description: Create a new rubric (lecturer/admin only).

get.description:
List all public rubrics (available to all authenticated users).

This endpoint is useful for students to browse available rubrics
and for lecturers to discover shared rubrics.

post.summary: Import Rubric From Pdf With Ai

get.description:
Get a specific rubric by ID.

Permissions:
- Public rubrics: Visible to all authenticated users
- Private rubrics: Only visible to creator or admin

put.description:
Update a rubric.

Permissions:
- Only creator or admin can update

delete.description:
Delete a rubric.

Permissions:
- Only creator or admin can delete

get.description:
Get rubric with nested items and level descriptions.

Permissions:
- Public rubrics: Visible to all authenticated users
- Private rubrics: Only visible to creator or admin

patch.description:
Toggle rubric visibility between public and private.

Permissions:
- Only creator or admin can toggle visibility

post.description: Duplicate a rubric with all its items and level descriptions.
Batch delete resources by IDs.
Batch update resources by IDs.

Import data from CSV/JSON format.

Supports importing Users, Classes, Units, Rubrics, Tasks, and other models.
Data is validated before import and bulk operations are used for efficiency.

get.description: Export data to CSV/JSON format.
Retrieve the public feed of shared essays.

post.description: Share a submission to the social hub.
Like, bookmark, or comment on a shared essay.
Report inappropriate content in the social hub.
Retrieve detailed analytics for student progress, class performance, etc.

get.description: Export analytics as CSV or PDF report.

post.description: Disable, enable, reset password, or force logout a user.

get.description: Get the activity log for a specific user.
Search for help articles.
Get a specific help article by its slug.

post.description: Create a new support ticket.

get.description: Retrieve the current user's support tickets.
